invariants.name: Idempotency
invariants.evidence: Concurrent create did not produce more than one payment ID.

invariants.name: FSM transitions
invariants.evidence: Webhook replay and reconciliation did not regress terminal state in the observed run.

invariants.name: Replay safety
invariants.evidence: Duplicate webhook deliveries were not observed to mutate state twice.

invariants.name: Persistence atomicity
invariants.evidence: Memory mode cannot fully prove DB-level atomicity.

invariants.name: PSP isolation
invariants.evidence: Harness only uses target interface; no domain coupling observed.

attack_results.scenario: parallel CreatePayment with identical idempotency key and payload
attack_results.expected_behavior: one logical payment should be created for identical merchant + idempotency key + payload

attack_results.scenario: replay attacks skipped due to missing payment ID
attack_results.expected_behavior: Need a payment to replay against
attack_results.notes: Create step failed or target returned empty payment ID.

attack_results.scenario: reconciliation skipped due to missing payment ID
attack_results.notes: Create step did not produce a payment ID.

attack_results.scenario: reset target state to simulate crash recovery boundary
attack_results.expected_behavior: Crash recovery must preserve invariants or be explicitly unsupported
attack_results.notes: Crash hook executed; post-reset state inspected.

critical_breaks.title: duplicate payment creation under concurrency
critical_breaks.root_cause: idempotency is not enforced atomically or is not keyed correctly
critical_breaks.financial_impact: same logical payment can be created more than once
reproduction_steps: call CreatePayment concurrently with same merchant, idempotency key and payload
observe more than one payment ID returned
critical_breaks.why_critical: This permits duplicate financial intent and breaks the core invariant of the payment system.
